verses.text: God goes before you as a consuming fire to subdue the nations. He goes. Victory is His doing.
God goes before you as a consuming fire to subdue the nations. He goes. Victory is His doing.
God goes before you as a consuming fire to subdue the nations. He goes. Victory is His doing.
Say not 'for my righteousness'; it is for their wickedness and His promise. Not your merit. Grace, not desert, gives the land.
Say not 'for my righteousness'; it is for their wickedness and His promise. Not your merit. Grace, not desert, gives the land.
Say not 'for my righteousness'; it is for their wickedness and His promise. Not your merit. Grace, not desert, gives the land.
Remember how you provoked God in the wilderness. Remember. They were ever rebellious.
Remember how you provoked God in the wilderness. Remember. They were ever rebellious.
Remember how you provoked God in the wilderness. Remember. They were ever rebellious.
Remember how you provoked God in the wilderness. Remember. They were ever rebellious.
At Horeb God would have destroyed them for the calf. The calf. Sin nearly undid them.
At Horeb God would have destroyed them for the calf. The calf. Sin nearly undid them.
At Horeb God would have destroyed them for the calf. The calf. Sin nearly undid them.
At Horeb God would have destroyed them for the calf. The calf. Sin nearly undid them.
Moses broke the tables when he saw their sin. Broken. The covenant was shattered.
Moses broke the tables when he saw their sin. Broken. The covenant was shattered.
Moses broke the tables when he saw their sin. Broken. The covenant was shattered.
Moses fell down forty days; God was angry with Aaron too. Interceded. He pleaded for the people.
Moses fell down forty days; God was angry with Aaron too. Interceded. He pleaded for the people.

He recounts their rebellions at Taberah, Massah, and Kadesh. Rebellions. They were prone to sin.

Moses prayed God spare them for His name and His people. The prayer. He appealed to God's glory.
